stocks.recent_news: M&M reported record FY26 results: consolidated revenue Rs 1,98,639 Cr (+25% YoY), PAT Rs 17,099 Cr (+35% YoY) - the highest in group history. Q4 FY26 revenue +29% to Rs 54,982 Cr; PAT +42% to Rs 4,668 Cr. Dividend Rs 33/share (+30%). Auto sales: 11.17 lakh vehicles (+19%) with #1 SUV position at >25% market share. Tractor volumes hit historic 5 lakh first time, market share >43%. Stock jumped 1.2% to Rs 3,145 on results day. April 2026 monthly sales: 56,331 auto units (+8% YoY, -6.5% MoM); 48,411 tractors.
stocks.sector_context: Indian auto sector strong in April 2026: Maruti Suzuki posted record monthly sales (1,87,704 units, 42% PV share); Tata Motors PV +30.5% YoY; Mahindra +8% YoY. Tata + Mahindra combined PV share has risen from 18% (FY22) to 27% (FY26) - tightening competitive intensity. EV growth strong (Tata EVs +72% YoY). Rural demand and SUV cycle remain primary tailwinds; auto tariff overhang globally a watch-item.
stocks.peer_comparison: M&M maintained #1 SUV share but trailed Maruti and Tata in April 2026 monthly volume momentum (M&M -6.5% MoM vs Maruti record month, Tata steady #2). FY26 full-year results clearly beat consensus. Tractor dominance (43%+ share) is unchallenged vs Escorts Kubota. Among auto OEMs, M&M's full-year financial print is best-in-class on margin and revenue growth.
red_flags: April 2026 monthly auto volumes -6.5% MoM - momentum easing
Stock has rallied sharply into results; risk of sell-on-news after dividend
EV ramp-up capex/competition (Tata EVs +72% YoY) could pressure consolidated margins
catalysts: FY26 results-day call commentary on FY27 guidance and EV roadmap

stocks.recent_news: BHEL hit all-time high of Rs 387.60 on May 4, 2026 (first ATH in 18 years), surged ~13% post Q4 results. Q4 FY26 PAT Rs 1,290 Cr vs Rs 504 Cr YoY (+156%); revenue Rs 12,310 Cr vs Rs 8,993 Cr (+37%). EBITDA margin expanded to ~14% from ~9%. FY26 revenue Rs 33,782 Cr (vs Rs 28,339 Cr); PAT Rs 1,282.7 Cr (2.5x FY25's Rs 512.97 Cr). Order inflows Rs 75,000 Cr in FY26; outstanding order book Rs 2.4 trillion. Final dividend Rs 1.40/share recommended.
stocks.sector_context: PSU capital goods/power equipment sector in strong upcycle: India's thermal capacity addition push (~80 GW by 2032), grid modernisation, and nuclear orders are fueling multi-year order book visibility for BHEL/L&T/Siemens. Defence and capex PSU theme remains in favour with mutual fund flows.
stocks.peer_comparison: BHEL's 14% EBITDA margin and 2.5x PAT growth represent a clear execution turnaround - leading PSU capital goods peers on operating leverage. The Rs 2.4 trillion order book provides ~7x revenue cover, among the best in the segment. Stock's 18-year ATH break signals re-rating of the broader PSU power equipment basket.
red_flags: Stock at all-time high after sharp 13% post-result spike - elevated profit-booking risk
BHEL margins historically volatile across cycles; sustainability of 14% EBITDA margin not yet proven
Heavy dependence on government/PSU thermal tenders - execution and payment cycle risk
catalysts: FY27 order pipeline commentary and large NTPC/state thermal awards
Final dividend Rs 1.40/share - ex-date and AGM

stocks.recent_news: Tata Technologies reported Q4 FY26 (May 4, 2026): consolidated PAT Rs 204.17 Cr (+8.1% YoY); revenue Rs 1,572.22 Cr (+22.29% YoY). Operating EBITDA Rs 252.1 Cr (+8% YoY) but EBITDA margin compressed to 16% from 18.2% YoY. Final dividend Rs 8.35 + one-time special Rs 3.35 = Rs 11.70/share total. Earnings call held 7 PM IST May 4 with management.
stocks.sector_context: Engineering R&D services sector facing pressure: Q4 QoQ growth among ER&D players modest at -0.8% to +1.6% in dollar terms. Demand uncertainty from elevated US tariffs adding pressure to automotive vertical exposure (Tata Tech's largest end-market). Improvement expected in 2H FY26 as new deal wins ramp.
stocks.peer_comparison: Margin trajectory weak vs peers: KPIT was the only ER&D peer to expand EBIT margin (+60bps to 17.3% YoY); Tata Tech, Cyient, LTTS, and Tata Elxsi all saw margin declines. Kotak/Choice prefer KPIT for SDV-led transformation positioning. Tata Tech's 16% EBITDA margin lags KPIT's 17%+ EBIT and Tata Elxsi historical levels - peer-relative underperformer on profitability.
red_flags: EBITDA margin compression to 16% from 18.2% YoY - peer-relative weakness
Heavy automotive vertical exposure amid global auto tariff pressure
PAT growth (+8%) lagging revenue growth (+22%) signals weakening operating leverage
Modest Q4 QoQ growth across ER&D peer set
catalysts: Final + special dividend Rs 11.70/share - ex-date and AGM
FY27 deal-wins ramp (management guidance on earnings call)
Recovery in automotive ER&D spending in 2H FY26

stocks.recent_news: Adani Green surged ~6% on May 4, 2026, trading around Rs 1,290.70 NSE, leading Adani group rally (Ports/Power also at new highs). Q4 FY26 PAT Rs 397 Cr (+72.61% YoY); sales Rs 3,328 Cr (+9.08%). FY26 PAT Rs 1,987 Cr; revenue Rs 11,602 Cr (+22.19%). Moody's upgraded AGEL-RG1 and AGEL-RG2 to 'Ba1/Stable' from 'Ba1/Negative' - clear credit positive. Promoter group declared NO new share encumbrances for FY26 (62.43% holding remains unpledged), filed April 3, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Renewable energy sector benefiting from India's push toward energy transition; tariff bidding remains competitive. Adani group as a whole regaining institutional favour in 2026 with ratings upgrades and post-Hindenburg recovery; ports and power subsidiaries hit new highs same day, suggesting return of momentum to the basket.
stocks.peer_comparison: Among large-cap renewables (vs Tata Power Renewable, ReNew, JSW Energy), Adani Green's PAT growth (+72%) is among the strongest. Moody's rating upgrade differentiates AGEL credit profile favourably from peers. Group-level rally implies better risk-on sentiment vs prior surveillance era.
red_flags: US SEC proceedings against promoters remain an overhang (no new May 2026 development found)
High leverage typical of renewable IPP business model
Stock has rallied sharply into results - momentum-driven move
catalysts: Further Moody's/S&P rating actions on remaining restricted groups

stocks.recent_news: HAL surged 6% to Rs 4,578.90 intra-day on May 4, 2026; closed around Rs 4,567.90. Stock has rallied ~31% since early April 2026 on defence theme strength. Leadership change: Kota Ravi took over as CMD effective May 1, 2026 (30+ years HAL experience, Tejas LCA programme). Recent order: Rs 2,901 Cr allocation for 6 ALH Mk-III Maritime helicopters with logistics support. Q4/FY26 audited results board meeting scheduled May 14, 2026. Mutual fund stake rose to 5.84% (March 2026) from 5.73% (Feb), with net Rs 272 Cr inflows.
stocks.sector_context: Indian defence sector in strong multi-year tailwind: FY27 defence budget Rs 7.85 lakh Cr; Nifty India Defence Index +6% in March 2026 alone. Top 5 defence PSUs (HAL, BEL, BDL, Mazagon Dock, L&T precision) hold combined order book of ~Rs 15 lakh Cr. Government export target Rs 50,000 Cr by FY29 supports order pipeline. Defence stocks recently extended rally with BEL, BDL, Mazagon, Solar Industries hitting new highs.
stocks.peer_comparison: HAL trades at ~29x FY28E EPS (post-Covid historical average) - fair vs sector. BEL FY25: 17% revenue growth, 34% PAT growth, debt-free, 29% RoE. Mazagon Dock debt-free, 34% RoE. HAL maintains leadership in fixed-wing aerospace; analyst target Rs 4,875 (BUY) implies ~7% upside from current. Defence basket broadly outperforming Nifty.
red_flags: Stock up ~31% in one month - elevated momentum/profit-booking risk
Management transition (new CMD May 1, 2026) - execution continuity to be watched
Defence PSU valuations (P/E >30x common) at upper end of historical bands
Q4 results 9 days away - run-up may already price in beat
catalysts: Q4 FY26 / FY26 results on May 14, 2026 - dividend announcement likely
New Tejas Mk-1A and LCH Prachand follow-on orders from MoD
FY27 export contracts under Rs 50,000 Cr target
First strategic priorities/communication from new CMD Kota Ravi
